meta.homeTitle: Great Lantern Festival — Not hosting in 2026
meta.homeDescription: Great Lantern Festival will not be hosted in 2026. Share interest via contact@greatlantern.com for future team roles and sister Mid-Autumn events such as APIDA Night Lights.
meta.teamDescription: Share interest in joining the Great Lantern Festival team for other events we're planning, or helping with sister Mid-Autumn events such as Sac State APIDA Night Lights.
meta.aboutDescription: About Great Lantern Festival — Tết Trung Thu, festival origins, and the autumn 2026 pause at Derr-Okamoto Park.

event.name: Great Lantern Festival
event.tagline: interest signups open · supporting sister Mid-Autumn events

hero.headline: Not hosting in 2026
hero.intro: Rooted in Tết Trung Thu — the Vietnamese Mid-Autumn celebration — Great Lantern Festival is a cherished night when families gather under the full moon: children carry lanterns, share mooncakes, and drink tea. We love this tradition and will return when we can host it well.
hero.subhead: We will not be hosting Great Lantern Festival at Derr-Okamoto Park this year.
hero.body: We're still gathering interest for our team — for other events we're planning, and to help with sister Mid-Autumn activations hosted by partner organizations, such as Sac State APIDA Night Lights. Email contact@greatlantern.com if you'd like to stay involved.

apply.intro: Tell us how you'd like to help — other events we're planning, or sister Mid-Autumn activations such as APIDA Night Lights. Browse the lanes below if you want a sense of the skills we look for.
steps: Email contact@greatlantern.com with your name, preferred contact method (email, text, or phone), and a line on how you'd like to help.
Mention skills or festival lanes that interest you, and any events you're already committed to this season (including partner hosts).
We'll follow up within a few business days and connect you where there's a fit.
apply.emailNote: No Idealist application this season — email is the way in.

directorIntro.title: Lanes we recruit for
paragraphs: These are the director and committee lanes we use when we produce Great Lantern Festival — and the skill areas we look for when supporting partner Mid-Autumn events.
A director is the go-to person for one festival area. You plan the work — volunteers, members, vendors, or paid contractors may execute it. You don't have to do every shift or task yourself.
Because we are not hosting GLF in 2026, these roles are not open seats for a Derr-Okamoto weekend — they are a map of how we work, and where your interest can still land this season.
notes: Interest this autumn may land on sister events (for example Sac State APIDA Night Lights) or other activations our team supports.
Co-chairs encourage counterparts across festivals — same lane, different producer — to bounce ideas and share learnings.
When we host again, Stage owns tent, sound, and physical stack; Program & Entertainment owns the master schedule and MC; show directors own content lanes.

productionMeetings.title: Stay connected
productionMeetings.intro: We're not running a full Derr-Okamoto production calendar this year — if you'd like to join a conversation about season support or future planning, reach out.
productionMeetings.zoom: To join a guest call, email contact@greatlantern.com.

teamPage.headline: Stay involved
teamPage.lead: We're not hosting Great Lantern Festival in 2026 — but we're still taking interest for our team and for help with sister Mid-Autumn events.

lanes.title: Site & operations
lanes.subtitle: Infrastructure modules
lanes.intro: Physical infrastructure, finance, and day-of execution. Vendor revenue funds this baseline.

roles.title: Finance & Administration
roles.test: How can one keep festival money honest through a live event weekend — booth collections, county permit pass-through, and closeout under real pressure?
roles.ship: Budget actuals current · service-vendor bills paid · Eventeny reconciliation and deposit closeout by October
roles.fit: Are detail-oriented and comfortable tracking money in and out

roles.title: Foundation
roles.test: How can one build a solid base of operations so every other lane can play?
roles.ship: Insurance, CSD permit, ops tents, and contingency signed off before load-in
roles.fit: Like building the platform others run on — checklists, deadlines, and make-it-official work

roles.title: Operations & Logistics
roles.test: How can one choreograph everyone so they know how to get to the site and load-in without causing a traffic jam?
roles.ship: Site map, load-in/out plan, and day-of ops radio for Fri load-in through Mon load-out
roles.fit: Love maps, timelines, and where does the truck go?

roles.title: Security
roles.test: How can one ensure safety for everyone on a sliding scale of available funds — and plan for contingency conditions?
roles.ship: Guard schedule scaled to budget, contingency plan, and Golden Protection liaison (Fri load-in + Sat festival 4–9 PM)
roles.fit: Are calm under pressure; can right-size coverage to the budget, plan for what-if scenarios, and liaise with Golden Protection

roles.title: Amenities
roles.test: How can one keep guests comfortable so they can stay as long as they want?
roles.ship: Restrooms, ADA unit, hand-wash placement, and porter service locked before gates open
roles.fit: Think about guest comfort, site flow, and keeping units stocked through Sat close

roles.title: Power & Lighting
roles.test: How can one power the park safely — load math, generators, electrical zones, and stage hookups dialed before gates open?
roles.ship: Generators and light towers installed, stage power and electrical zones live — capacity and hookups cleared for Light Up the Park
roles.fit: Understand power math and safety — generators, light towers, load planning, diesel logistics; coordinate with Light Up on decorative loads

lanes.title: Program & experience
lanes.subtitle: What families see and remember
lanes.intro: Stage, schedule, and signature moments — including sponsor-funded features from Fund The Festival.

roles.title: Stage
roles.test: How can one ship an MVP stage stack — tent, portable sound, lion dance — on a community budget?
roles.ship: Stage tent, portable sound, and Saturday programming incl. lion dance
roles.fit: Have production or AV experience (helpful, not required)

roles.title: Program & Entertainment
roles.test: How can one keep the public entertained while keeping entertainment participants on time?
roles.ship: Saturday run-of-show, MC scripts, and talent cues locked with Stage before event week
roles.fit: Love show flow for a family crowd, building relationships with performers, and keeping talent on schedule without losing the audience — ready to be the programming foundation show directors build on

roles.title: Lantern Procession
roles.test: How can one create a moment families — especially children — will remember?
roles.ship: One dusk walk-through Sat evening — LED lanterns, music, volunteer staging
roles.fit: Love ritual moments and can stage volunteers without micromanaging

roles.title: Children's Village
roles.test: How can one keep children entertained through interaction, rather than walk-and-see — without it turning into chaos?
roles.ship: Village open Sat — anchor hosts, crafts, meetup corral · bounce houses and inflatables when funded (vendor COI + Foundation CGL)
roles.fit: Think like a school picnic organizer — do stations and anchor hosts, not empty tents to walk past

roles.title: Children's Show
roles.test: How can one book and stage family sets — magic, storytelling, puppetry — that hold kids' attention in 15–20 minute blocks?
roles.ship: Talent booked · 15–20 min sets run-of-show to Program & Entertainment before event week
roles.fit: Have booked youth or family entertainment before (helpful, not required) — you own a show module; Program owns the master clock

roles.title: Honor our Elders
roles.test: How can one offer tea hospitality so every elder feels honored — not only grandparents in groups, but also someone who showed up alone?
roles.ship: Shade tent, seating, and tea station staffed before Sat peak
roles.fit: Understand hospitality as respect — Mid-Autumn is for honoring elders, whoever walks in

roles.title: Light Up the Park
roles.test: How can one design and install the park's dusk atmosphere — uplights, string lights, stage flowers — so the vibes land for procession hour?
roles.ship: Uplights, string lights, stage flowers live for procession hour · loads coordinated with Power & Lighting
roles.fit: Have an eye for atmosphere and hands-on setup — vibe design and decorative rigging; Power installs generators and light towers

lanes.title: Outreach & partners
lanes.subtitle: Audience and ally modules
lanes.intro: Director filled? Full lane details still apply — inquire to join as a Member.

roles.title: Sponsorship
roles.test: How can one create win-win-win situations — great guest experiences, producers who make it happen, and sponsors growing their business?
roles.ship: Registry pipeline active · asks out · sponsor deliverables tracked through event weekend
roles.fit: Think in win-win-win — guests get memorable experiences, producers get resources to ship them, sponsors get real business growth

roles.title: Marketing & Communications
roles.test: How can one create the most effective approach for getting the word out — and gathering public feedback that feeds into the design of future events?
roles.ship: Email and social through event week · save-the-dates, registry posts, week-of rally · feedback log for post-mortems
roles.fit: Think in two-way comms — reach families where they are, and listen to what they tell you for the next event

roles.title: Vendor Operations
roles.test: How can one create a winning balance of vendor profitability and guest experience — families looking for great tastes?
roles.ship: Eventeny live — applications processed, category caps enforced, county permit pass-through, waitlist through event weekend
roles.fit: Care about food-market economics and guest experience — curated composition, fair process, comfortable saying no when a category is full

roles.title: Volunteers
roles.test: How can one create a great experience for volunteers — and help them see the value of their contributions?
roles.ship: Recruitment, shifts, waivers, appreciation · shift leads execute · contractor fallback if critical slots won't fill
roles.fit: Think about volunteer experience and recognition — honest shifts, clear roles, appreciation that shows people their work mattered

roles.title: Partnerships & Grants
roles.test: How can one position Great Lantern Festival as a pillar of the community-at-large — and build mutually beneficial relationships with every organization around it?
roles.ship: Partnership map · CCSD liaison · grant renewals · cross-promo allies · outreach log (confirmed vs exploratory) · Fund The Festival registry stubs for grant-born ideas
roles.fit: Think in mutual benefit across the local ecosystem — not just sponsorship asks; comfortable building institutional relationships and advising on festival design; can give grant-born ideas basic registry shape before handing off to a lane director

phase2.title: Sponsor unlocks — Phase 2
phase2.intro: These lanes activate when sponsors fund the matching Fund The Festival gift. Plan with the base lane lead in the meantime; hold the vision until the block unlocks.
roles: Stage+ Director — How can one upgrade to concert-quality sound and basic stage lights beyond MVP?
Security+ Director — How can one add guards at peak hours when sponsors fund the block?
Amenities+ Director — How can one scale restrooms and sinks for a busy Saturday crowd?

Spread the Word Director — Book 1–2 parent-lane influencer Reels, run paid Meta/boost on the winner, and pitch Good Day Sacramento (one backup TV channel). Registry: $1K Reels + $800 boost + $500 TV coordinator stipend.

faq.q: Are you hosting Great Lantern Festival in 2026?
faq.a: No — we will not host GLF at Derr-Okamoto Park in 2026. We're still taking interest emails for future team roles and for help with sister Mid-Autumn events (such as Sac State APIDA Night Lights).
Email contact@greatlantern.com — name, preferred contact method, and a line on how you'd like to help. No Idealist posting this season.

faq.q: Why list director lanes if you're not hosting?
faq.a: They're a map of how we organize when we produce GLF — and the skill areas we look for when supporting partner events. Share which lanes interest you when you email.

faq.q: Should I find a counterpart at another festival?
faq.a: Yes — we encourage it. Every organizer benefits from someone running the same lane at another festival or event. Co-chairs can help make an intro if you don't have one yet.
Helpful, not required. Co-chairs and peer organizers back you up.

These are volunteer leadership roles when we host — you plan the lane; others may execute. Sponsor-funded experiences may include modest stipends for performers or vendors — not director compensation.

faq.q: What's the difference between site & ops and program?
faq.a: Site & ops keeps the gates open, power on, and guests comfortable. Program & experience is what families come for — stage, procession, children's programming, and elder hospitality.

sections.title: Tết Trung Thu
paragraphs: Mid-Autumn Festival, known as Tết Trung Thu in Vietnamese culture, is a cherished celebration when families gather under the full moon. Children carry lanterns, share mooncakes, and drink tea — a night of reunion, gratitude, and hope.

For Vietnamese communities in America, the festival reconnects generations and keeps cultural traditions alive in a new homeland. The lantern procession — children walking together at dusk — is the signature moment families remember.

sections.title: Elk Grove, 2026
paragraphs: We will not host Great Lantern Festival at Derr-Okamoto Park in 2026. The festival line continues — we're focusing this autumn on supporting sister Mid-Autumn celebrations and gathering interest for our team.

When we host again, GLF remains a free, family-centered afternoon and evening with food vendors, stage programming, and lantern procession at dusk, in partnership with Vietnamese American Community of Sacramento (VACOS), Community Partners Advocate of Little Saigon Sacramento (cPALSs), Norcal Vietnamese Chamber of Commerce (NVCC), and Cosumnes Community Services District (CCSD).

sections.title: Origins of this festival line
paragraphs: This festival line began in 2021 as Mid-Autumn Moon Celebration — a NVCC and cPALSs project at Florin Towne Centre on Florin Road in Sacramento's Little Saigon corridor. The first year was presented by those two organizations alone; VACOS was not yet a co-host.

In 2022 the same hosts returned for a second annual Mid-Autumn Multicultural Festival and Night Market at Florin. In 2023 the celebration moved to Laguna Town Hall in Elk Grove, and VACOS joined NVCC and cPALSs as a co-host — the tri-host coalition that has carried the festival since.

Great Lantern Festival continues that spiritual line — the same community-built Mid-Autumn tradition, now at Derr-Okamoto Park under a broader coalition that still carries the founding partnership of NVCC and cPALSs.

meta.description: Custom Zones at Great Lantern Festival 2026 — your name on the map. Decorate your corner yourself or ask us to help bring it to life.

hero.tagline: Your name on the map. Your community in the crowd.

diy.label: I want to decorate it myself

help.label: Help me bring it to life
help.mailtoSubject: GLF 2026 — Custom zone (help bringing it to life)

vendor.label: I just want a booth spot

diySection.title: Decorate it yourself
diySection.intro: You get a named corner of the festival — yours to decorate as you see fit. We assign the footprint on the map. You bring popup shade, your look, volunteers, and programming for your community. Baseline BYO pricing is below; add-ons quoted separately.

helpSection.title: Help me bring it to life
helpSection.intro: Pick only what you need — we quote shade, tables, identity, lighting, hosted activities, and microstage on top of your zone. Festival staff install and strike rigged items.

storiesSection.title: Examples
storiesSection.intro: What it can look like — not price quotes.

caseStudies.title: Open mic, coffee-shop vibes
caseStudies.persona: Music club or songwriter circle — acoustic sets, not a main-stage slot
caseStudies.summary: String lights, café tables, and a small plaza stage — members take five-minute sets while the crowd lingers with boba and acoustic sets.

caseStudies.title: Toddler parents' meetup
caseStudies.persona: Parents' group — ages 1–4, busy families who need a clear home base
platformProvides: Shade, tables, and chairs on arrival
Your group's name on the map
caseStudies.summary: Shade, tables, and toddler activities ready when families arrive — bubble play, story mat, and a map pin so nobody hunts for each other in the crowd.

caseStudies.title: Filipino Village
caseStudies.persona: Filipino cultural org — barangay-scale identity on the map
caseStudies.summary: Parol-inspired lanterns over the plaza, a welcome arch, and tinikling in the open — the map says Filipino Village, not a single vendor table.

hostActivations.addonsIntro: Add-ons stack in four tiers — decor, setup, hosted activities, and microstage. Rigged items (lighting, backdrop, PA) are install-inclusive — festival staff set up and strike; not for tenant self-install on popups. Partnerships quotes per deal; no public price list.

addonCategories.label: Identity & decor
addonCategories.description: Signage, welcome banner, photo backdrop, zone lighting — festival installs rigged decor; BYO popups are shade-only.

addonCategories.label: Shade & setup
addonCategories.description: Frame tent rental, **tables & chairs** (popular with parent groups), portable power.

addonCategories.label: Hosted activities
addonCategories.description: Crafts, story time, games, photo moments — optional facilitator slots.

addonCategories.label: Microstage setup
addonCategories.description: Portable PA + plaza seating — you bring performers. Festival organizers do not book zone talent for Great Lantern Festival 2026.

apply.title: Start your inquiry
apply.intro: Tell us about your organization — Partnerships will follow up within a few business days with a recommended setup. They may ask about shade, lighting, and artwork; you don't need answers on day one.

faq.q: What's a spot in a custom zone?
faq.a: One mapped 10×10 tent pad inside your zone. The baseline also includes the **plaza in front of the spot row** — open area for photos and mini programming. Bring popup shade (or add **Capitol frame tent** rental). A zone spot is not an assigned vendor spot.

faq.q: Can I BYO popup tents and still get zone lighting?
faq.a: Yes — popup shade is fine. String lighting installs on **Capitol frame tents**, a **festival pole kit**, or **ground uplights** — festival staff rig and strike; we do not hang lights on popups.

faq.q: Why rent a zone instead of three vendor spots?
faq.a: Three vendor registrations get you three commerce spots in the vendor ring. A custom zone gives you adjacent spots **plus the plaza in front** — a named corner where your community can linger.

faq.q: Do festival organizers book singers for my zone?
faq.a: Not for Great Lantern Festival 2026. Microstage add-ons cover portable PA and plaza seating only. You bring your own performers and run-of-show — karaoke crew, church singers, community talent.

faq.q: How is this different from showing up uninvited?
faq.a:
A custom zone is a **named corner on the festival map** — adjacent spots plus a plaza in front where your community can find you, decorate, and linger. You go through intake so load-in, insurance, and map placement are coordinated with the rest of the event.

Showing up without registering means no assigned footprint, no map pin, and no slot in the festival plan. The event runs Derr-Okamoto Park under a **special-event permit** with assigned vendor spots and custom zones. Unregistered setups in the festival footprint — including unauthorized food vendors — are not part of the layout. **Zone hosts do not personally enforce park rules or remove people from the park**; festival operations and park staff handle conflicts day-of.

faq.q: Can we rent a zone if we're not Vietnamese-led?
faq.a: Yes — activation zones are for any tribe with a community and an identity to express. Cross-community villages (e.g. Chinese, Filipino, Russian) are welcome.

logoDesign.headline: Logo Design
logoDesign.lead: Official design brief for Great Lantern Festival branding — a modular, highly wearable logo system rooted in Tết Trung Thu and this festival line's Little Saigon Sacramento origins.

meta.description: Official logo design brief for Great Lantern Festival — festival background, modular branding, Hội An lantern art, West Coast logotype, and proof-of-human-artistry audit trail.

homepageCta.title: Logo Design
homepageCta.body: We're commissioning a handmade logo system for Great Lantern Festival — modular branding rooted in Little Saigon Sacramento and Tết Trung Thu. Artists: read the brief and email us with questions or a proposal.

sections.title: Project Overview
paragraphs: Create a modular, highly wearable logo system for the Great Lantern Festival — the current name for this community's Mid-Autumn / Tết Trung Thu festival line. The design must celebrate the authentic identity of the Little Saigon Sacramento community, pairing a deep reverence for traditional Vietnamese roots with the rugged, homegrown street culture of Northern California.
The modular blocks below encode that history: Little Saigon Sacramento stays permanent (the spiritual home of the line); Elk Grove, California is the current event location and must remain swappable.

sections.title: Festival Background
paragraphs: Tết Trung Thu is the Vietnamese Mid-Autumn Festival — families gather under the full moon; children carry lanterns, share mooncakes, and drink tea. It is a night of reunion, gratitude, and hope. For Vietnamese communities in America, it reconnects generations and keeps cultural traditions alive in a new homeland. The lantern procession at dusk is the signature moment families remember.

Great Lantern Festival continues this festival line. We will not host a Derr-Okamoto Park weekend in 2026; when we produce again, it remains a free, family-centered afternoon and evening with food vendors, stage programming, and lantern procession at dusk, in partnership with Vietnamese American Community of Sacramento (VACOS), Community Partners Advocate of Little Saigon Sacramento (cPALSs), Norcal Vietnamese Chamber of Commerce (NVCC), and Cosumnes Community Services District (CCSD).

This festival line began in 2021 as Mid-Autumn Moon Celebration — a NVCC and cPALSs project at Florin Towne Centre on Florin Road in Sacramento's Little Saigon corridor. The first year was presented by those two organizations alone. In 2022 the same hosts returned for a second annual festival at Florin. In 2023 the celebration moved to Laguna Town Hall in Elk Grove, and VACOS joined as co-host — the tri-host coalition that has carried the festival since. Great Lantern Festival continues that spiritual line at Derr-Okamoto Park.

Designers should treat Little Saigon Sacramento as the permanent cultural anchor of the brand, even when the printed location block reads Elk Grove. Past season fliers (2021–2024) are on the Media page and show how host neighborhoods and partners evolved while the community identity stayed continuous.

orderedItems.label: The Fruit Crate Frame (The California Context)
orderedItems.text: Inspired by vintage 1930s–1950s Central Valley agricultural crate labels. It grounds the celebration in the region's agricultural landscape where the community established its roots.

orderedItems.label: The Main Art (The Vietnamese Anchor)
orderedItems.text: Centered entirely on the physical craftsmanship of the Hội An Silk Lantern (bamboo ribbed frame, fluid silk panel curves, and hanging tassel).

orderedItems.label: The Logotype (The West Coast Vibe)
orderedItems.text: A custom, hand-rendered West Coast script or sign-painter lettering that brings genuine Sacramento street energy.

sections.title: The Tripartite Modular Framework
paragraphs: The logo is built on a three-tier vertical block system. This allows the branding to be completely deconstructed for specific merchandise (like sports caps) or updated if the festival expands geographically.

frameworkBlocks.text: HARVEST MOON & HỘI AN LANTERN + HERO WEST COAST LOGOTYPE

bullets.label: Style
bullets.text: A crisp, unpolished sans-serif or trade-union stamp font.

bullets.label: Purpose
bullets.text: Stays permanent across all future iterations — the spiritual home of this festival line (founded on Florin Road in Sacramento's Little Saigon corridor) and an explicit distinction from Southern California's Little Saigon.

bullets.label: The Main Art
bullets.text: A bold, solid representation of the full harvest moon. Superimposed over it is the elegant silhouette of a Hội An silk lantern. The lantern's bamboo ribs must be visible, and its long tassel should drop down dynamically to interact with the text.

bullets.label: The Logotype
bullets.text: "Great Lantern Festival" rendered in a bold, custom-drawn West Coast font. It must be able to break completely free from the frame to live as a standalone asset for premium streetwear drops and headwear collaborations (e.g., 3D puff embroidery on caps).

bullets.label: Text
bullets.text: "ELK GROVE, CALIFORNIA" (balanced with the festival year).

bullets.label: Style
bullets.text: Matches the industrial block font of the top tier.

bullets.label: Purpose
bullets.text: Completely swappable — Elk Grove is the current home (Laguna Town Hall 2023–2024; Derr-Okamoto Park 2026), not the permanent cultural identity. If the festival hosts satellite events or pop-ups in other regional areas, the print shop can easily swap this block without touching the core art.

bullets.label: Aesthetic
bullets.text: Flat-ink, high-contrast screen-print look. The lines should feel hand-carved and have a heavy, tactile presence on fabric. No smooth digital gradients or neon glows.

bullets.label: Color Palette
bullets.text: Sun-baked California harvest tones. Warm saffron gold, deep terracotta/burnt orange, and an earthy charcoal or midnight indigo for the heavy outline work.

bullets.label: Merchandise Intention
bullets.text: The assets must look exceptional screen-printed on heavyweight 240GSM box-fit t-shirts, canvas tote bags, and embroidered heavy-fleece hoodies.

sections.title: Proof of Human Artistry & Audit Trail
paragraphs: To ensure absolute community trust and preserve the cultural integrity of the festival, the commissioned artist must provide an audit trail verifying the handmade nature of the artwork:

bullets.label: Process Recording
bullets.text: Continuous or step-by-step screen recordings (for digital illustration) or video timelapses (for traditional media) showing the art being drawn from initial rough sketch to final vector line work.

bullets.label: Layered Delivery
bullets.text: The final file must be delivered with its original, unflattened sketch and rough pencil layers intact beneath the final inks.

bullets.label: Marketing Utility
bullets.text: These process videos will be used across community social media channels to proudly showcase the human craftsmanship behind the festival's identity.

contact.title: Questions
